category_title: Reserved students who only share knowledge when directly prompted by an adult
scenario_title: Group observation and discussion of crabs
minimal_context: The student is participating in a group workshop session to observe and discuss crabs.

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Let's observe these crabs together and share your ideas and questions with the group.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Watches the crabs quietly while Deisha, Sierra, Abria, and Brandon do most of the talking.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: What else do you all notice?

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Looks at the crabs but remains reserved, not blurting out any ideas.)

dialogue.speaker: tutor
dialogue.message: Keep discussing what you see with each other.

dialogue.speaker: student
dialogue.message: (Stays quiet despite having good questions, waiting until she has the direct attention of an adult before speaking.)

dialogue_combined:
TUTOR: Let's observe these crabs together and share your ideas and questions with the group.

STUDENT: (Watches the crabs quietly while Deisha, Sierra, Abria, and Brandon do most of the talking.)

TUTOR: What else do you all notice?

STUDENT: (Looks at the crabs but remains reserved, not blurting out any ideas.)

TUTOR: Keep discussing what you see with each other.

STUDENT: (Stays quiet despite having good questions, waiting until she has the direct attention of an adult before speaking.)

task.content: Observing and discussing crabs... sharing ideas and asking questions

context.social_setting: Group work where peers (Deisha, Sierra, Abria, Brandon, and Alana) do most of the talking

challenging_behavior_trigger.condition: During group work where other participants are sharing ideas and asking questions
challenging_behavior_trigger.observable_behavior: Is more reserved... doesn’t just blurt ideas out as much

behavior_patterns.condition: When peers do most of the talking during group activity
behavior_patterns.observable_behavior: Is more reserved and seems only to ask her questions when she has the attention of an adult
